fundamentals.name: Bloom Energy Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. It offers Bloom Energy Server, an energy server platform to convert fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels, into electricity through a non-combustion electrochemical process. The company also provides Bloom Electrolyzer for producing hydrogen. It sells its products through direct and indirect sales channels to utilities, data centers, retail, healthcare, education, telecom, manufacturing, and other industries. The company was formerly known as Ion America Corp. and changed its name to Bloom Energy Corporation in 2006. Bloom Energy Corporation was incorporated in 2001 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with high multiples and significant leverage, showing strong revenue expansion but thin margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 242.4% (0.44 vs 0.13); next earnings date is 2026-04-28.
positives: Revenue growth 130.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current Q 286.1% and current FY 179.8% — strong future growth expected.
Last quarter EPS surprise of 242.4% (0.44 vs 0.13) indicates strong earnings beat.
Current ratio 5.03 and quick ratio 3.98 — healthy short-term liquidity.
Free cash flow (TTM) $265.52M — positive cash generation.
negatives: Forward P/E 64.15x — valuation is very high, pricing in substantial future growth.
Price/Sales (TTM) 32.21x and Price/Book 85.54x — premium valuation metrics.
Profit margin 0.2% and Operating margin 9.6% — margins are thin despite revenue growth.
Debt/Equity 311.48 — high leverage on the balance sheet.
Return on equity 1.3% — low profitability relative to shareholder equity.

news.paragraph: Bloom Energy (BE) has seen a surge in its share price, with recent articles highlighting its strong performance and potential as a future stock to buy. Despite some reports placing the stock in the red on a particular day, the overall sentiment from recent coverage is bullish, emphasizing its outperformance against the industry and significant analyst growth estimates. Traders are weighing whether the current surge has already priced in the optimistic outlook.
